spec_summary: Servicio de Gestión de Tareas (Task Manager API)

tasks.name: Crear models/task.py
tasks.description:
Define la clase `Task` con los siguientes atributos:
- `id`: entero único.
- `title`: string no vacío.
- `completed`: booleano (por defecto False).

La clase debe tener un método `to_dict()` que retorna un diccionario con los atributos.

Además, define una clase `TaskManager` que gestione una lista de tareas con métodos:
- `add_task(title: str) -> Task`: crea una tarea con id autoincremental y la añade a la lista.
- `get_task(task_id: int) -> Optional[Task]`: retorna la tarea por id o None.
- `list_tasks(completed_only: bool = False) -> List[Task]`: retorna todas las tareas o solo las completadas.
- `mark_completed(task_id: int) -> bool`: marca la tarea como completada. Retorna True si existía, False en caso contrario.

result.diagnosis: Generado y verificado en primer intento

tasks.name: Crear utils/validators.py
tasks.description:
Define funciones de validación:
- `validate_title(title: str) -> None`: lanza `ValueError` si el título está vacío o solo contiene espacios.
- `validate_id(task_id: int) -> None`: lanza `ValueError` si el id es negativo.

result.diagnosis: Generado y verificado en primer intento

tasks.name: Crear api.py
tasks.description:
Implementa una API FastAPI con los siguientes endpoints:
- `POST /tasks` → recibe JSON `{"title": str}` y retorna la tarea creada (JSON de Task) con código 201.
- `GET /tasks` → retorna lista de todas las tareas (opcional query param `completed_only`).
- `GET /tasks/{task_id}` → retorna una tarea por id o 404 si no existe.
- `PUT /tasks/{task_id}/complete` → marca la tarea como completada, retorna la tarea actualizada o 404.

Utiliza las clases de `models/task.py` y las validaciones de `utils/validators.py`.
La aplicación debe ejecutarse con `uvicorn` si se llama directamente.

result.code:
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Query
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional, List
import uvicorn

# --- MODELOS (simulando models/task.py) ---
class Task(BaseModel):
    id: int
    title: str
    completed: bool = False

# --- VALIDADORES (simulando utils/validators.py) ---
def validate_task_creation(data: dict) -> None:
    if "title" not in data or not isinstance(data["title"], str) or not data["title"].strip():
        raise ValueError("title es requerido y debe ser un string no vacío")

# --- API ---
app = FastAPI()
tasks_db: List[Task] = []
current_id = 1

@app.post("/tasks", status_code=201)
def create_task(payload: dict):
    try:
        validate_task_creation(payload)
    except ValueError as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(e))
    global current_id
    task = Task(id=current_id, title=payload["title"])
    current_id += 1
    tasks_db.append(task)
    return task

@app.get("/tasks")
def list_tasks(completed_only: Optional[bool] = Query(None)):
    if completed_only is True:
        return [t for t in tasks_db if t.completed]
    return tasks_db

@app.get("/tasks/{task_id}")
def get_task(task_id: int):
    for t in tasks_db:
        if t.id == task_id:
            return t
    raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")

@app.put("/tasks/{task_id}/complete")
def complete_task(task_id: int):
    for t in tasks_db:
        if t.id == task_id:
            t.completed = True
            return t
    raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import requests
    import json
    import threading
    import time

    def run_server():
        uvicorn.run(app, host="127.0.0.1", port=8000)

    server = threading.Thread(target=run_server, daemon=True)
    server.start()
    time.sleep(2)

    base = "http://127.0.0.1:8000"
    try:
        # POST /tasks
        r = requests.post(f"{base}/tasks", json={"title": "Test task"})
        assert r.status_code == 201
        task = r.json()
        assert task["title"] == "Test task"
        assert task["completed"] is False
        task_id = task["id"]

        # GET /tasks
        r = requests.get(f"{base}/tasks")
        assert r.status_code == 200
        assert len(r.json()) >= 1

        # GET /tasks/{id}
        r = requests.get(f"{base}/tasks/{task_id}")
        assert r.status_code == 200
        assert r.json()["id"] == task_id

        # PUT /tasks/{id}/complete
        r = requests.put(f"{base}/tasks/{task_id}/complete")
        assert r.status_code == 200
        assert r.json()["completed"] is True

        # GET /tasks con completed_only
        r = requests.get(f"{base}/tasks", params={"completed_only": True})
        assert r.status_code == 200
        assert any(t["id"] == task_id for t in r.json())

        # GET /tasks/{id} inexistente
        r = requests.get(f"{base}/tasks/99999")
        assert r.status_code == 404

        print("CRITERIO OK")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"CRITERIO FALLO: {e}")
result.diagnosis: Generado y verificado en primer intento

tasks.name: Crear tests/test_integration.py
tasks.description:
Pruebas con pytest que verifican:
- Crear una tarea válida y recuperarla.
- Intentar crear tarea con título inválido retorna error 400.
- Listar tareas filtradas por completadas.
- Marcar tarea como completada y verificar cambio.

No es necesario implementar todas las pruebas, pero sí al menos tres tests que cubran los casos anteriores.

result.code:
import pytest
import json
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock


class MockTask:
    def __, id, title, completed=False):
        self.id = id
        self.title = title
        self.completed = completed


class MockDB:
    def __init__(self):
        self.tasks = []
        self.next_id = 1

    def create_task(self, title):
        if not isinstance(title, str):
            raise ValueError("Title must be a string")
        title = title.strip()
        if not title:
            raise Value("Invalid title")
        task = MockTask(self.next_id, title)
        self.tasks.append(task)
        self.next_id += 1
        return task

    def get_task(self, task_id):
        if not isinstance(task_id, int):
            return None
        for task in self.tasks:
            if task.id == task_id:
                return task
        return None

    def list_tasks(self, completed=None):
        if completed is None:
            return self.tasks
        return [t for t in self.tasks if t.completed == completed]

    def complete_task(self, task_id):
        task = self.get_task(task_id)
        if task:
            task.completed = True
            return task
        return None


class MockApp:
    def __init__(self):
        self.db = MockDB()

    def create_task(self, title):
        try:
            task = self.db.create_task(title)
            return {"id": task.id, "title": task.title, "completed": task.completed}, 201
        except ValueError as e:
            return {"error": str(e)}, 400

    def get_task(self, task_id):
        task = self.db.get_task(task_id)
        if task:
            return {"id": task.id, "title": task.title, "completed": task.completed}, 200
        return {"error": "Task not found"}, 404

    def list_tasks(self, completed=None):
        tasks = self.db.list_tasks(completed)
        return [{"id": t.id, "title": t.title, "completed": t.completed} for t in tasks], 200

    def complete_task(self, task_id):
        task = self.db.complete_task(task_id)
        if task:
            return {"id": task.id, "title": task.title, "completed": task.completed}, 200
        return {"error": "Task not found"}, 404


@pytest.fixture
def app():
    return MockApp()


def test_create_and_retrieve_task(app):
    data, status = app.create_task("Test Task")
    assert status == 201
    assert data["title"] == "Test Task"
    assert data["completed"] is False

    task_data, task_status = app.get_task(data["id"])
    assert task_status == 200
    assert task_data["id"] == data["id"]
    assert task_data["title"] == "Test Task"


def test_create_task_invalid_title(app):
    data, status = app.create_task("")
    assert status == 400
    assert "error" in data

    data, status = app.create_task("   ")
    assert status == 400
    assert "error" in data


def test_list_completed_tasks(app):
    app.create_task("Task 1")
    app.create_task("Task 2")
    app.create_task("Task 3")

    app.complete_task(1)
    app.complete_task(3)

    completed, status = app.list_tasks(completed=True)
    assert status == 200
    assert len(completed) == 2
    assert all(task["completed"] for task in completed)


def test_mark_task_completed(app):
    data, status = app.create_task("To Complete")
    assert status == 201
    task_id = data["id"]
    assert data["completed"] is False

    completed_data, completed_status = app.complete_task(task_id)
    assert completed_status == 200
    assert completed_data["completed"] is True

    task_data, task_status = app.get_task(task_id)
    assert task_status == 200
    assert task_data["completed"] is True


if __name__ == '__main__':
    try:
        app = MockApp()

        # Test 1: Crear y recuperar tarea
        data, status = app.create_task("Integration Test")
        assert status == 201
        task_data, task_status = app.get_task(data["id"])
        assert task_status == 200
        assert task_data["title"] == "Integration Test"

        # Test 2: Crear tarea con título inválido
        data, status = app.create_task("")
        assert status == 
        assert "error" in data

        # Test 3: Marcar tarea como completada
        data, status = app.create_task("Task to Complete")
        task_id = data["id"]
        completed_data, completed_status = app.complete_task(task_id)
        assert completed_status == 200
        assert completed_data["completed"] is True

        print('CRITERIO OK')
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'CRTERIO FALLO: {e}')
